extraction.one_line_summary: PIDCOCK Tom attacked with 10.5km remaining on Alpe d'Huez, winning on Tour debut while ROGLIČ did domestique damage dropping multiple GC riders including BARDET.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ working as super-domestique on Alpe d'Huez proves Jumbo willing to burn GC podium for VINGEGAARD's yellow security.
POGAČAR attacked 2-3 times on Alpe d'Huez despite previous day's collapse, showing recovery capacity contradicts traditional stage race fatigue patterns.
Breakaway gap manipulation: under 5min at 50km-to-go, then grew again on descent showing peloton's strategic tolerance when VINGEGAARD secure.
PIDCOCK descending masterclass on Galibier allowed him to bridge from chasing group to leaders, revealing positioning weakness before KOM summits.

rider_signals.PIDCOCK Tom: positive: attacked with 10.5km remaining on Alpe d'Huez debut, won convincingly showing grand tour climbing legs immediately available
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: worked as domestique for VINGEGAARD dropping QUINTANA/GAUDU/BARDET, confirming podium sacrifice for team yellow defense
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: attacked multiple times day after 3-minute loss, recovery rate faster than expected suggesting metabolic resilience
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: dropped twice on Alpe d'Huez but regained contact both times, moved to third overall demonstrating superior pacing discipline
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: dropped by ROGLIČ tempo work losing podium position, confirms inability to follow sustained high-altitude efforts
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: recovered well after 8-minute loss previous day, finished with GC group suggesting one-off crisis rather than form collapse
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: made breakaway and finished third in break group, first front-of-race presence since 2019 crash indicating partial return
